articles.title: Bengaluru Weather Today: Karnataka Districts To See Rains In Next 2-3 Days, Get Relief From Rising Temperature
articles.full_text:
Bengaluru Weather Today: Karnataka Districts To See Rains In Next 2-3 Days, Get Relief From Rising Temperature
Bengaluru Weather Today: Bengaluru and several parts of Karnataka have been reeling under hot and humid conditions, with temperatures staying above normal over the past few days. However, there's some relief in sight as the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has forecast rainfall in multiple districts across the state later this week.
For those who are checking for updates related to the weather in Bengaluru and other districts in Karnataka, here are all the details to know.
Bengaluru Weather Today
Bengaluru is currently witnessing its peak temperature in a year. Minimum temperature in Bengaluru is expected to be close to 24 degrees Celsius and the maximum temperature is estimated to be close to 24 degree Celsius in Bengaluru on Tuesday, April 21
As per IMD report, Bengaluru is likely to witness "mainly clear sky with maximum and minimum temperature very likely to be around 36 ° C and 24 ° C respectively." The maximum temperature in the city is likely to remain above normal by 2-3 degree Celsius.
The Karnataka capital is likely to witness partly cloudy weather this week with minimum and maximum temperatures hovering close to 36 degree Celsius and 24 degree Celsius. The city can witness some decline in temperature in the second half of the week.
Will It Rain in Bengaluru This Week?
It is unlikely that there will be rainfall in Bengaluru this week, as the IMD has predicted only partly cloudy weather and mild humidity in the city.
Karnataka Weather Forecast: IMD Predicts Rainfall In Multiple Districts
The IMD has predicted "light rain/thundershowers at one or two places over Dakshina Kannada and Udupi districts," on Wednesday, April 22. Hot and humid conditions are likely to prevail at isolated pockets over Dakshina Kannada, Udupi and Uttara Kannada districts.These places are expected to see mild rainfall and shower throughout the week.
North Interior Karnataka districts like Belagavi, Dharwad, Haveri, Gadag, Kalburgi, Yadgir and Raichur districts may also see rainfall this week. In south interior Karnataka, Shivamogga, Chikkamagaluru, Kodagu and Hassan districts may see light rainfall and thundershowers in the second half of the week.
Large parts of Karnataka are bracing for an unusual mix of weather, with the India Meteorological Department warning of a continuing heatwave even as several districts prepare for rain, thunderstorms and possible hailstorms from April 20. Day temperatures may climb close to 45°C in some areas, but short, intense spells of rain and strong winds are likely to break the heat in selected regions.
